stories.title: The Rainy Day
stories.story:
It was Saturday morning, and Ali woke up excited to play football in the garden. But when he looked out the window, he saw dark clouds and heavy rain. "Oh no," he said. "I can't play outside today."

Ali sat by the window, feeling bored and a little sad. His mother noticed and smiled. "Why don't you read a book instead?" she suggested. Ali wasn't sure at first, but he walked to the bookshelf and picked an old, dusty book about a brave dog named Rex.

The story was about Rex, who lived in a small village. One night, a fire started near the village while everyone was asleep. Rex smelled the smoke and barked loudly until the whole village woke up and escaped safely. Ali couldn't stop reading — he wanted to know what happened to Rex next.

By the time Ali finished the book, the rain had stopped and the sun was peeking through the clouds. He looked outside, ready to play football, but then he smiled and picked up the book again. "Maybe just one more chapter," he said. Ali had discovered that rainy days could be exciting too — as long as you had a good story.

stories.title: The New Student
stories.story:
On Monday morning, a new girl named Mona joined Layla's class. Mona sat alone at lunch because she didn't know anyone yet, and she looked nervous.

Layla remembered how scary it felt when she was new at a different school last year. Nobody had talked to her that first day, and she had felt very lonely. Layla didn't want Mona to feel the same way.

So Layla walked over to Mona's table with her lunch tray. "Hi, I'm Layla. Can I sit with you?" she asked. Mona's face lit up with a big smile. They talked about their favorite games and discovered they both loved drawing.

By the end of the week, Mona wasn't the "new girl" anymore — she was Layla's best friend. Layla learned that a small act of kindness, like sitting with someone, can change their whole day.
